step1_project_intake.product: Hourglass — Slow Sand / Fast Sand / Heavy Sand (3 series)
step1_project_intake.market: Global (Korea origin, global lifestyle channel target)
step1_project_intake.target_customer: 30–50s, wellness + collecting dual pursuit, adults who buy ritual
step1_project_intake.brand_goal: AI creative portfolio + public case study asset
step1_project_intake.confirmed_pov: We don't sell hourglasses. We sell the weight of a moment.
design_principles: Put the act at the center
anti_direction: No productivity language: focus, Pomodoro, efficiency, mindful, productive
No excessive minimalism: erasing material physicality with cleanliness

step3_clusters.weakness: Difficult new customer entry, weak digital channel
Cannot expand to luxury, weak material depth, cold mechanical impression
Cannot position as luxury, no brand depth, low collectible value

step3_clusters.story_type: Silent luxury, material is the brand
Everyday as ritual — focus, meditation, wellness
step3_clusters.weakness: Productivity tool frame limitation, cannot expand to luxury

step3_clusters.story_type: Intersection of architecture, art, and design
step3_clusters.weakness: No mass accessibility, no utility, distribution limitation
Weak global positioning, unclear brand story depth

step4_positioning_maps.whitespace_description: No brand occupies Emotional + Mid-Minimal with a functional permanent object at $150–$400. Aesop/Byredo are closest but are consumables, not collectible objects.

map_a.description: GRAIN in Ritual area, Future position. Heritage Craft brands at Ritual+Heritage. Ugmonk/MetaEDC at Function+Future. Only GRAIN can claim Ritual+Future.

map_b.description: GRAIN at intersection of Weight and texture — refined yet living material. No competitor in this space.

map_c_recommended.description: GRAIN in Emotional area, Minimal-Mid position. Sensory brands occupy emotion space but are consumables. GRAIN differentiates as functional permanent object. Clearest whitespace.

primary_target.behavior: Conscious consumption, values object history, prefers physical experience over SNS
primary_target.purchase_tendency: Buys less but invests in quality, values collectible worth

key_selection_criteria.description: Is this really brass and glass? No cheap feel?
key_selection_criteria.brand_language: The sand is proof. The brass is proof. The glass is proof.

key_selection_criteria.description: Must still be on the desk in 5 years
Does the act of turning itself become a special moment?

avoidance_criteria: Productivity language — Pomodoro timer, focus tool, efficiency → immediate exit
Excessive minimalism — empty background, perfect render, fingerprint-free surface → factory product feel
Gift shop impression — Amazon delivery box, gift set bundle, mass retail image → purchase refusal
preferred_visual_language: Late afternoon (4PM) natural light + window shadows
Compositions where hands are visible (hands as protagonist, product as supporting)
Brass fingerprints, glass bubbles, individual sand grains — imperfection as proof
Muted warm neutral background (Cloud Dancer to Angora range)
preferred_message_structure: Assertion: This is the weight of 30 minutes.
Paradox: We made something slow, in an age that wants everything fast.
Material description: 3,600 seconds of sand. One brass chamber. Infinite iterations of now.
Act description: Turn it. The moment you've been waiting for begins.
purchase_triggers: Visual shock — glass refraction or brass close-up photo
Gift justification — birthday/anniversary/promotion gift spending reason
Space fit — lifestyle image triggering desk imagination
share_triggers: ASMR sand falling video — highest Save + Share rate
Carousel knowledge content (Physics of Hourglasses / Why Brass Changes)
Unboxing experience — weight of package opening moment
step5_preference_analysis.heritage_spectrum_position: Preserve material heritage (brass/glass/sand), speak about it in present language. Does not sell the past; speaks the physical truth of material in today's language.
strategic_implications: Target thinks long before purchase but has very low post-purchase regret — First impression visuals are everything
Gift positioning lowers entry barrier while reducing price resistance
Luxury consumers prioritize Brand Story over Functional Specs — material narrative and production process are core

whitespace.description: Triangle: Material Luxury + Practical Price + Ritual Language
whitespace.material_authenticity: Brass + glass at Carl Auböck level but in contemporary language
whitespace.ritual_positioning: Ritual language: implementing Rituals brand language as objects
whitespace.global_dtc: Korean origin but English-first global communication

unmet_target_needs: Practical object to sense time without productivity language — currently none
Premium hourglass in brass+glass at $150–$400 — currently none
Hourglass with collectible series structure — currently none
Content-type premium object discoverable on SNS — currently none
entry_rationale: Category gap: No global D2C brand in premium (>$150) functional hourglass market
Timing: Slow luxury trend + ASMR/sensory content culture intersection
Material advantage: Brass+glass has highest AI image generation quality
Collecting culture: 1 purchase → 3-set completion psychology → LTV naturally rises
step6_market_gap_positioning.positioning_statement: GRAIN is the only hourglass that earns its place on your desk not by measuring time, but by making you feel it.
step6_market_gap_positioning.brand_promise: We make objects that turn the abstract into material — time into weight, a moment into gravity, sand into proof.

prompt_A_studio.role_objective: Luxury product photographer for GRAIN. Single hero shot communicating material weight, tactile richness, quiet luxury without lifestyle context.
prompt_A_studio.layout_geometry: Center composition, 15 degree tilt. Hourglass 60% frame. Camera slightly elevated 10 degrees. 40% breathing room below.
prompt_A_studio.environment_lighting: Warm ambient fill from large left window source (45 degrees). Directed spot from directly above for crisp shadow pool. Rim light from behind-right through glass — internal glow effect. No flash.
prompt_A_studio.physics_blending: Sand in mid-fall — half passed. Individual grains visible in macro. Brass shows hairline micro-scratches (aged first-use patina). Glass reveals one micro-bubble. Proof of material.
prompt_A_studio.human_touch_layer: No human. Single fingerprint smudge on brass lower chamber (slightly out of focus, bottom-right) — evidence of use.

prompt_B_surreal.role_objective: Surrealist art director for GRAIN. Stop scroll by placing hourglass in unexpected but emotionally resonant environment.
prompt_B_surreal.layout_geometry: Widescreen 16:9. Hourglass at human scale (~1.5m) on salt flat. Shot from waist height, slight upward tilt. Hourglass in left-third. Right two-thirds: open sky amber-to-pale-blue-grey.
prompt_B_surreal.environment_lighting: Golden hour. Long dramatic shadow extending right. Sand inside casts amber light onto salt flat — warm circle of light on ground. Wind-blown dust partially obscures base.
prompt_B_surreal.physics_blending: Sand inside appears made of same salt particles as ground — inside/outside boundary ambiguous. Brass fully oxidized green-bronze (patina at maximum). Glass slightly fogged.
prompt_B_surreal.human_touch_layer: No human. Single hand-sized impression in salt flat beside hourglass base — as if someone knelt here before shot.
negative_constraints: NO CGI-smooth sand (must look like real photography with grain)
Must feel like found photograph not rendered visualization

prompt_C_lifestyle.role_objective: Lifestyle photographer capturing the exact moment of ritual — the turning of GRAIN hourglass. Subject is the act, not the object. Hand is protagonist.
prompt_C_lifestyle.layout_geometry: Vertical 4:5 format. Hand enters from right, fingers curled around upper brass chamber, mid-turn. Hourglass at 90 degree tilt. Camera: eye-level to hand, shallow depth of field f/1.8.
prompt_C_lifestyle.environment_lighting: Late afternoon (4PM) natural light through window — warm orange cast. Long horizontal shadow of window frame crossing desk. Desk: aged oak with visible wood grain. Background: unfocused books, ceramic cup, soft warm blur.
prompt_C_lifestyle.physics_blending: Sand caught mid-suspension — frozen between two states. Some grains clinging to glass wall; others beginning their fall. Skin and brass occupying same temperature visually.
prompt_C_lifestyle.human_touch_layer: Hand of a 40-year-old — not young, not aged. Short clean nails. No rings. Slight crease on palm. Someone who does things deliberately.
negative_constraints: NO ambient tech (no phone, laptop, screen glow)
NO lifestyle overcrowding (one object, one hand, one act)
Brass must never look gold — brass, aged, used, honest

step8_gate1_checklist.confirmed_pov: We don't sell hourglasses. We sell the weight of a moment.
step8_gate1_checklist.pov_necessity: All competitors position as time measurement (function) or decoration (appearance). Nobody uses making you feel time physically — experience-centered language. This gap is GRAIN's entry basis.

different_belief_from_competitors.competitor_belief: Hourglasses are tools for measuring time or decorative objects
different_belief_from_competitors.grain_belief: Hourglasses are physical evidence of time. The act of watching sand fall is the experience itself.

target_emotional_response: Right — I am not buying a tool. I am buying this weight.
If I put this on my desk, it expresses who I am.
Have you ever watched 30 minutes with your eyes? This is that 30 minutes.
step8_gate1_checklist.recommended_map: Map C (Functional to Emotional × Minimal to Expressive)
step8_gate1_checklist.whitespace_description: $150–$400 / Ritual language / Global D2C — no incumbent brand

declining_trends: Cold Scandinavian minimalism (empty background + perfect solo shot)
AI perfect render image overuse — fingerprints/irregularities signal premium

GRAVITY.essence: Material weight, patina, aging. Things that deepen the longer they exist.

SILENCE.essence: System recognizable without logo. Sans-serif, matte, monochrome palette.

ACT.essence: The moment of turning is the center of brand experience.
